etymology.description: semantic 糹(糸) (mì, "silk; thread") + phonetic 丩(丩) (jiū, "to entangle") -> concept of correcting
etymology.interpretation: In the character 糾, the silk radical 糹(糸) represents something that can be tangled or arranged, reflecting the idea of adjusting or correcting, while the phonetic component 丩(丩) contributes the sound and also originally meant 'to entangle', hinting at the process of resolving issues by 'correcting' entanglements.

examples.chinese: 他们在会上讨论如何纠正错误(他們在會上討論如何糾正錯誤)。
examples.pinyin: Tāmen zài huìshàng tǎolùn rúhé jiūzhèng cuòwù.
examples.english: They are discussing how to correct mistakes at the meeting.

examples.chinese: 老师帮助学生纠正发音(老師幫助學生糾正發音)。
examples.english: The teacher helps students correct pronunciation.

examples.chinese: 他尝试纠正自己的态度(他嘗試糾正自己的態度)。
examples.pinyin: Tā chángshì jiūzhèng zìjǐ de tàidù.
examples.english: He tries to correct his own attitude.
